item.title: Dante #8: Hell Cantos 20-32

Transcript

Okay. Um, good morning. Um, welcome back. So, there are a couple of outstanding issues from last class that I would like to uh address. So, Carol's son uh who was here, he had a question about what is difference between pride and ego. And this is a very hard question. Okay. So first let's think about think about what the ego is. So in the cabala

tradition uh and the cabala tradition is very much um influenced by the divine comedy. If you learn the cabala and you read the divine comedy you see a lot of similarities and these are not coincidental but in the cabala tradition um there is divine spark in everyone but it's covered by the husk. Okay, the husk you can say it's the body, it's the ego, but the but for the Gabala tradition the ego is basically just the body and the desire to fulfill the body which means

material pleasures. Okay, so in the Cabala tradition that's what the ego means. Dante doesn't see it that way. Um for Dante the ego is really the divine spark. Okay? In that it can propel us to uh good things and to bad things. So it's potentiality as opposed to just good or bad. Okay? It's it's potential potentiality. Uh and the ego can lead us astray. And when it leads us to astray,

it's because of pride. Okay? And so for Dante, pride is an issue because it wants you. It wants um the other egos to subordinate to your ego. All right? So, it's one of superiority, a feeling of superiority where you are above others. And for Donnie, that's a problem because that limits your access to God. Okay?

Love is about equality. It's about uh being egotarian and treating others with respect and trusting others. And the ego, the pride, it's about um elevating yourself above others. Okay. And the classic example of pride in the mind comedy is Virgil, right? Virgil. [snorts] Okay. So that's the difference for um in most religious traditions there's really they don't really separate the two. Ego

is pride but for Dante there is a difference. Ego is potentiality. It's possibility and pride is a perversion of the ego. Okay. The ego should lead should lead you towards faith, hope and love. And without the ego you could not actually go on this path. But um if you go on the wrong path, it leads you to pride. Okay. And again uh in the in the divine comedy even though it is not explicit uh Virgil is the manifestation the expression of pride. Okay. And we can see that in his actions

um where um he uh but it'll become more obvious as as we go as we go along. Okay. So that's the first issue. Does does it make sense to everyone? Okay. Uh second issue is the issue of homosexuality. Okay. And this is an issue that raised concerns um so one question that I was asked yesterday is okay um what's the difference between homosexuality and heterosexuality? Okay.

So so it so so thank you for for phrasing that that question. And I went back and I thought about this and even though my explanation it's okay, it's not truthful enough because the way for us to understand truth is AAM's razor. Okay, Aam's razor is the idea of elegance. Meaning I should be very I should be able to pretty simply and clearly explain the idea and I and I was not able to do that yesterday. It raised a lot of questions. It raised a lot of

doubts. So even though we were approaching the truth, we were not at the truth. So let me today try to more clearly and simply explain the problem of of of homosexuality. Okay? And so um yesterday I made things a bit complicated. I brought in the idea of incest and that was very confusing. Okay? Let's not do that. Okay? Let's just like stick with Dante. So with Dante, the key to understanding him is to first appreciate the punishment at work. Okay? Because for Dante, the

punishment is God's perfect justice. Okay? So we need to understand the punishment first. And the punishment for these homosexuals is they are in a plains where like there's raining missiles on them. Okay? And so they have to dodge these missiles. For homosexuals, they have to do it in a group. Okay, they have to do it in a group and if one of them strays from the group, that person is punished harder. Now, this is interesting to think about because

why would homosexuals be punished in such a manner? Okay, so now we we think about let's not think about what they did wrong, but if the punishment is you have to stick together as a group and you're being punished as a group and if you fall away from the group, you're punished even harder. What kind of people should be punished like that? Right? If we knew what the punishment is and we understand that these punishments

are reflection of God's perfect justice, then what kind of people, what did you do wrong in society that would make God punish you like this? Uh yes. >> Uh they think they think they're like superior to the to the society as a general. Like they're they feel like they're entitled. So they're they feel like they're above like uh like the normal people. >> Good. Okay. So that's getting somewhere. Okay. But again, what do they do wrong?

So if in that case the male they don't need a female and female also don't need a male too. So maybe they're enemy of each other. >> Right. Right. Let's not think about like their homosexual. super self-centered, you want to force them to be in a group and think as a group. >> Okay, that makes sense. Okay. Yeah.
